quoteFirst: And again at
quoteLast: a report says: “The _Duke of Edinburgh_ had now taken station astern of the _Shannon_, the battle cruisers were engaged and had wheeled to starboard. Leading ships of the 2nd Cruiser Squadron were starting to cross the bows of the Battle Fleet from port to starboard. Battle cruisers firing intermittently, light cruisers making their way through the destroyer flotillas to attack the enemy light cruisers.”
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935
